title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 32 points with eight assists, leading the Celtics to a 114-99 win over the Charlotte Hornets.
Payton Pritchard contributed 28 points and six assists, capitalizing on pick-and-roll plays against Hornets defenders.
Ron Harper Jr. scored seven points before twisting his ankle, while Jordan Walsh started and provided strong defense.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Celtics secure a playoff berth for the 12th consecutive season after defeating the Hornets 114-99.
Jaylen Brown, sidelined with left Achilles tendinitis, celebrated the Celtics' 50 wins in a social media post.
Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 32 points, leading the Celtics despite the absence of key players Brown and White.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early has a 2.19 ERA in his first five career starts with the Boston Red Sox.
In his first 2026 season start, Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run and striking out six.
Though the Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Reds, Early's strong performance may secure his rotation spot.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet clinched a Walter Cup Playoffs berth by defeating the Minnesota Frost 4-2 at Grand Casino Arena.
Fleet has a record of 13-5-2-4 and are the first team to secure a playoff spot in the 2026 season.
Netminder Aerin Frankel and rookie Amanda Thiele contributed to Fleet's performance in the season and recent victory.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, is charged with manslaughter after a fatal stabbing in a South Boston apartment.
The incident occurred Friday evening at 258 Old Colony Ave; the victim died in the hospital the next day.
Pascual's charges were upgraded from assault with a dangerous weapon to manslaughter; arraignment set for South Boston District Court.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police officers shot a 44-year-old man in the shoulder during a pursuit after he allegedly fired at them.
A 71-year-old man was grazed by a bullet while sleeping in a nearby home during the exchange of gunfire.
Both injured men were taken to hospitals with non-life threatening injuries; investigation by State Police detectives is ongoing.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights less than a month before the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
John Tortorella will replace Cassidy as head coach of the Golden Knights, who are third in the Pacific Division.
Vegas has struggled recently, losing six of their last seven games, with goalie depth being a significant issue.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots projected to draft Clemson's T.J. Parker, a defensive end, with the 31st pick in the NFL Draft.
Parker recorded 126 tackles, 21.5 sacks, and six forced fumbles over three seasons at Clemson.
New England seeks defensive reinforcements after losing K'Lavon Chaisson and signing Dre'Mont Jones.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Newburyport police investigate thefts of historical plaques, including one from Old Hill graveyard's African American section.
Recent plaque thefts in Newburyport include locations such as Oak Hill Cemetery, Cashman Park, and Atkinson Common.
Police seek public assistance and collaboration with nearby jurisdictions, urging residents to report any relevant information.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year entry-level contract for the 2026-27 season.
Max Lundgren, 23, from Angelholm, Sweden, led Merrimack to their first Hockey East title, earning tournament MVP honors.
Lundgren set Merrimack records for saves and minutes played, posting a .920 save percentage in 39 games last season.
